title: paspalum gardnerianum
intro: Paspalum gardnerianum, also known as the "Makalani Palm," is a type of tropical grass native to the Pacific Islands, characterized by its slender leaves and feathery plumes. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Paspalum gardnerianum include reducing inflammation, promoting skin healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, or salves, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions.
